commit.message:
feat: full ownership of leads — remove all source/role restrictions

Once a lead enters the system (from Trello or ManyChat), it belongs
to the agency. Any user with leads write permission can now:
- Edit any field of any lead regardless of source (Trello/ManyChat)
- Delete any lead regardless of who it's assigned to or where it came from
- Move/reassign leads freely in the Kanban

Remaining data integrity constraint (kept intentionally):
- Cannot delete a lead that is linked to an operation

Protected system fields (never overwritten):
- id, created_at, external_id, trello_url

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix scrape: call Railway backend directly, bypass Vercel proxy timeout

Vercel serverless functions have a 10s timeout (free plan) which kills
the scrape proxy before it completes. Now calls NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL
directly for scraping, with 3min timeout per city.

commit.message:
Add OSM real-data ingestion and fix Census BPS-to-H3 county join

- notebooks/07_ingest_osm_competitors.py: new notebook that queries the
  OpenStreetMap Overpass API (free, no key) for real QSR competitor
  locations and POI (grocery, retail, office, school, entertainment,
  healthcare). Supports competitors_source=osm|synthetic and
  poi_source=osm|synthetic widgets. Caches Overpass responses in
  /Volumes/{catalog}/bronze/raw/osm_cache/. Schema-compatible with
  bronze.competitors and bronze.poi so all Phase 2 notebooks run
  unchanged.

- resources/phase1_osm_ingestion.yml: DABs job that runs notebook 07
  with quality-check tasks for both competitors and poi tables.

- notebooks/06_build_dev_signal_layer.py: replace national-average
  BPS fallback with Census ZCTA-to-County 2020 Relationship File
  (public download, ~11MB). Per-zip county_fips lookup gives proper
  spatial permit signals instead of a flat national average applied
  to all 33k+ US zip codes.

- databricks.yml: add competitors_source and poi_source bundle variables.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add AI-powered product search service

Introduces a new product-search-service implementing a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented
Generation) pipeline using Amazon Bedrock, S3 Vector Object Store, and DynamoDB.
Demonstrates Datadog LLM Observability with distributed tracing across the full
async write path (EventBridge → SQS → Bedrock Titan embed → S3 Vectors) and
synchronous read path (POST /search → embed → vector search → Claude generation).

Key capabilities:
- Natural language product search via Bedrock Claude 3.5 Haiku
- Automatic product catalogue indexing from 5 EventBridge event types
- Full Datadog LLM Observability: embedding + generation spans in a single trace
- Span links, DSM checkpoints, and OTel messaging semantic conventions
- Proactive indexing of pre-existing products on pricing/stock events

Infrastructure: Python CDK, SAM, and Terraform variants
CI/CD: GitHub Actions workflow with smoke + pipeline integration tests
Runtime: Python 3.13, arm64 Lambda

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: spinner floods terminal with identical lines on SSH pseudo-TTYs

The build spinner printed ~10 frames/sec regardless of whether anything
changed. On SSH pseudo-TTYs where cursor-up escape doesn't work, each
frame created a new line — producing 25+ identical "Building ... [0"
lines before compilation started.

Fix: only redraw when compiled_count or phase actually changes. Also
hide "[0 crates]" during fetch/download phase (count only shown once
compilation starts). Result: one line per new crate instead of flooding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: program status UI, external-ON runs, Run button live state

1. Program execution status bar: shows program name, step progress
   (e.g. 2/3. zóna) and filled/empty dot indicator above zone cards.
   Zone cards show 'Program' or 'Manuális' label when ON.

2. External-ON countdown: _on_state(ch, 1) now creates a failsafe
   SprinklerRun (FAILSAFE_MAX seconds) when no active run exists for
   that sprinkler, so externally-started zones show a countdown.

3. Run button live state: any_zone_on is computed in partial_zones()
   and pushed to the dashboard's Run button via hx-swap-oob on every
   3-second poll — no manual refresh needed.

4. Program tracking: jobs.py calls set_current_program() before
   run_sequentially and clear_current_program() in finally; each step
   advances the counter via on_run_start callback.

    201  #scrollTopBtn
    300  .sync-topbar (was 9999)
    500  #dd-backdrop (was 9998)
    501  .dropdown    (was 9999)

commit.message:
Add link to technical paper in How to Use section

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(schema): change Field.name from String to Arc<str> (#6351)

Field is cloned frequently during query planning and schema operations.
Each clone previously heap-allocated a new `String` for the name, but
field names are never mutated after construction. `Arc<str>` makes
clones O(1) (atomic refcount bump, no allocation) and shrinks Field by 8
bytes (2 words vs 3).

Core changes:
- `Field.name: String` -> `Arc<str>`
- `Field::new/rename bounds: Into<String>` -> `Into<Arc<str>>`
- `Schema.name_to_indices: HashMap<String, _>` -> `HashMap<Arc<str>, _>`

Downstream fixes across 60 files follow mechanical patterns:
- `.name.as_str()` -> `.name.as_ref()`
- `.name.clone()` where `String` needed -> `.name.to_string()`
- `Field::new(&string_ref, ..)` -> `Field::new(s.as_str(), ..)`
- `HashSet<String>.contains(&f.name)` -> .`contains(&*f.name)`
- `assert_eq!(field.name, "lit")` -> `assert_eq!(&*field.name, "lit")`

PyO3 boundary still returns `String` to Python. Serde, Display, Hash,
and PartialEq semantics are unchanged (all compare str content).

Closes #2892.

commit.message:
Add vector-valued VIE-1 solver (D extension)

- New D entry point volterra_solve_vie1_vec handles (N,d,d) matrix kernels
  and (N,d) vector g/solution arrays for any d.
- solve_VIE_1_vec_impl!(cd,cc,d): fully compile-time specialised for d=1..8,
  with fixed-size stack arrays and lin_solve!(d*m) fully unrolled.
- solve_VIE_1_vec_runtime_impl!(cd,cc): runtime-d LAPACK path for d>8;
  meson.build updated to detect and link liblapack with Have_lapack flag.
- volterra_solve_vie1 is now a thin wrapper calling volterra_solve_vie1_vec
  with d=1; scalar path is unchanged.
- force_continuous=True is fully supported for both compile-time and runtime d.
- Python: solve_vie1_vec_d() in _dlang.py; solve_VIE_1() in solvers.py
  accepts 3-D kernel_values (N,d,d) and 2-D g_values (N,d) on the vector
  path; 2-D kernel now raises ValueError with a clear message.
- 59/59 tests pass, including 3 new vector VIE-1 tests.

unlocks Step 2, clears any previous RAID output.
Delete button: removes from list and storage, does not clear current context.
Reset now clears all projects.

Legacy API (saveProjectState etc.) preserved and aliased. 368 tests passing.

commit.message:
fix: Skip push when local meals empty and server has no new data

Prevents a device that has lost its meal data from silently overwriting
the server's meal history with an empty blob. The guard in pushData()
now checks: if local meals = {} AND the inline pull found no server
changes (pulled=false), skip the push entirely and log a warning.

When the paired device syncs, the server timestamp advances past our
SYNC_LAST_PULL_KEY, the next inline pull picks up the 170 meals,
we merge them into local storage, and then push the merged data.

Also removed unreachable dead code after the return in syncNowDetailed().

commit.message:
fix(admin): remove nested dynamic import causing loading hang

The layout.tsx wrapped ClientShell in dynamic() with ssr:false, and
ClientShell itself had another dynamic() for ClientShellContent.
This double-dynamic nesting caused the login page to hang on "Loading..."
because the outer dynamic import had to resolve the entire module chain
(including wagmi provider imports) before the /login path check could run.

Changed to a direct import of ClientShell in layout.tsx — the inner
dynamic() for ClientShellContent still handles the wagmi code-split.
Also added error.tsx boundary to surface future runtime errors.

commit.message:
Fix context menu breaking after viewport item reactive re-render

When kernel.attach/detach modified the viewport item non-silently,
the reactive system routed it through rerenderItem (morphdom path).
morphdom preserves old event handlers, but viewport-view's handlers
have closures over body-appended elements and document listeners
that get destroyed by cleanup — causing the context menu to silently
write to a detached element.

Route viewport item re-renders through renderViewport() (full
teardown + fresh render) instead of morphdom, which avoids the
entire class of stale-closure issues.
